001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.parquet;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.marshall.parquet.ParquetSchemaElement.*;
022
023import java.time.*;
024import java.util.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
030
031/**
032 * Builds Parquet schema (list of SchemaElement) from Juneau bean ClassMeta.
033 *
034 * <p>
035 * Maps Java types to Parquet physical and logical types per the
036 * <a class="doclink" href="https://parquet.apache.org/docs/file-format/">Parquet specification</a>.
037 */
038@SuppressWarnings({
039   "java:S1192" // Duplicated "value" is Parquet Optional key; constant would obscure schema mapping
040})
041public final class ParquetSchemaBuilder {
042
043   private final MarshallingContext marshallingContext;
044   private final boolean writeDatesAsTimestamp;
045   private final ParquetCycleHandling cycleHandling;
046   private final int maxRecursionDepth;
047   private final boolean nativeLogicalTypes;
048
049   /** Fixed scale/precision for INT64-backed native DECIMAL emission (GAP-9). 9 fractional digits, 18 total. */
050   static final int DECIMAL_SCALE = 9;
051   static final int DECIMAL_PRECISION = 18;
052
053   /**
054    * Constructor.
055    *
056    * @param marshallingContext The bean context for type resolution.
057    * @param writeDatesAsTimestamp If true, Date/Calendar/Temporal map to INT64+TIMESTAMP_MILLIS; else to BYTE_ARRAY+STRING.
058    * @param cycleHandling How to handle structural cycles when building schema.
059    */
060   public ParquetSchemaBuilder(MarshallingContext marshallingContext, boolean writeDatesAsTimestamp, ParquetCycleHandling cycleHandling) {
061      this(marshallingContext, writeDatesAsTimestamp, cycleHandling, 5, false);
062   }
063
064   /**
065    * Constructor.
066    *
067    * @param marshallingContext The bean context for type resolution.
068    * @param writeDatesAsTimestamp If true, Date/Calendar/Temporal map to INT64+TIMESTAMP_MILLIS; else to BYTE_ARRAY+STRING.
069    * @param cycleHandling How to handle structural cycles when building schema.
070    * @param maxRecursionDepth Maximum expansion depth for self-referential bean types (GAP-13).
071    */
072   public ParquetSchemaBuilder(MarshallingContext marshallingContext, boolean writeDatesAsTimestamp, ParquetCycleHandling cycleHandling, int maxRecursionDepth) {
073      this(marshallingContext, writeDatesAsTimestamp, cycleHandling, maxRecursionDepth, false);
074   }
075
076   /**
077    * Constructor.
078    *
079    * @param marshallingContext The bean context for type resolution.
080    * @param writeDatesAsTimestamp If true, Date/Calendar/Temporal map to INT64+TIMESTAMP_MILLIS; else to BYTE_ARRAY+STRING.
081    * @param cycleHandling How to handle structural cycles when building schema.
082    * @param maxRecursionDepth Maximum expansion depth for self-referential bean types (GAP-13).
083    * @param nativeLogicalTypes If true, emit binary-native DECIMAL/DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP-micros logical types
084    *    instead of the default string / TIMESTAMP-millis normalization (GAP-9/10).
085    */
086   public ParquetSchemaBuilder(MarshallingContext marshallingContext, boolean writeDatesAsTimestamp, ParquetCycleHandling cycleHandling, int maxRecursionDepth, boolean nativeLogicalTypes) {
087      this.marshallingContext = marshallingContext;
088      this.writeDatesAsTimestamp = writeDatesAsTimestamp;
089      this.cycleHandling = cycleHandling != null ? cycleHandling : ParquetCycleHandling.NULL;
090      this.maxRecursionDepth = Math.max(1, maxRecursionDepth);
091      this.nativeLogicalTypes = nativeLogicalTypes;
092   }
093
094   /**
095    * Builds the Parquet schema for the given bean class.
096    *
097    * @param cm The bean class metadata.
098    * @return The depth-first list of schema elements (root first).
099    */
100   public List<ParquetSchemaElement> buildSchema(ClassMeta<?> cm) {
101      return buildSchema(cm, null);
102   }
103
104   /**
105    * Builds the Parquet schema, using a sample bean to resolve collection element types when generics are erased.
106    *
107    * @param cm The bean class metadata.
108    * @param sampleBean Optional sample instance; when provided, collection element types are resolved from actual values.
109    * @return The depth-first list of schema elements (root first).
110    */
111   public List<ParquetSchemaElement> buildSchema(ClassMeta<?> cm, Object sampleBean) {
112      var elements = new ArrayList<ParquetSchemaElement>();
113      var typesInProgress = new HashMap<Class<?>, Integer>();
114      addSchemaElements(elements, cm, "root", null, true, sampleBean, typesInProgress);
115      return elements;
116   }
117
118   /**
119    * Builds the Parquet schema for a Map root (single row, columns from map keys).
120    *
121    * @param map The map instance (keys become column names).
122    * @return The depth-first list of schema elements (root first).
123    */
124   public List<ParquetSchemaElement> buildSchemaFromMap(Map<?, ?> map) {
125      var elements = new ArrayList<ParquetSchemaElement>();
126      var keys = map.keySet().stream()
127         .filter(String.class::isInstance)
128         .map(k -> (String) k)
129         .sorted()
130         .toList();
131      elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(
132         "root",
133         null,
134         null,
135         null,
136         keys.size(),
137         null,
138         null,
139         null,
140         null,
141         null));
142      var typesInProgress = new HashMap<Class<?>, Integer>();
143      for (var key : keys) {
144         var val = map.get(key);
145         var cm = marshallingContext.getClassMeta(val != null ? val.getClass() : Object.class);
146         addSchemaElements(elements, cm, key, "root", false, null, typesInProgress);
147      }
148      return elements;
149   }
150
151   /**
152    * Builds the Parquet schema for a Map root with non-String keys (flat list-of-pairs: root.key, root.value).
153    *
154    * <p>
155    * Each map entry becomes one Parquet row. Keys and values use native Parquet types.
156    *
157    * @param keySample Sample key for type inference.
158    * @param valueSample Sample value for type inference.
159    * @return The depth-first list of schema elements (root first).
160    */
161   public List<ParquetSchemaElement> buildSchemaForKeyValuePairs(Object keySample, Object valueSample) {
162      var elements = new ArrayList<ParquetSchemaElement>();
163      var typesInProgress = new HashMap<Class<?>, Integer>();
164      var keyCm = marshallingContext.getClassMeta(keySample != null ? keySample.getClass() : Object.class);
165      var valueCm = marshallingContext.getClassMeta(valueSample != null ? valueSample.getClass() : Object.class);
166      elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement("root", null, null, null, 2, null, null, null, null, null));
167      addSchemaElements(elements, keyCm, "key", "root", false, null, typesInProgress);
168      addSchemaElements(elements, valueCm, "value", "root", false, null, typesInProgress);
169      return elements;
170   }
171
172   private void addSchemaElements(List<ParquetSchemaElement> elements, ClassMeta<?> cm, String name, String parentPath, boolean isRoot, Object sampleBean, Map<Class<?>, Integer> typesInProgress) {
173      if (cm.isOptional()) {
174         addOptionalSchema(elements, cm, name, parentPath, isRoot, sampleBean, typesInProgress);
175      } else if (cm.isBean()) {
176         addBeanSchema(elements, cm, name, parentPath, isRoot, sampleBean, typesInProgress);
177      } else if (cm.isByteArray()) {
178         addLeafSchema(elements, cm, name, parentPath, isRoot);
179      } else if (cm.isCollection() || cm.isArray()) {
180         addListSchema(elements, cm, name, parentPath, isRoot, sampleBean, typesInProgress);
181      } else if (cm.isMap()) {
182         addMapSchema(elements, cm, name, parentPath, isRoot, typesInProgress);
183      } else {
184         addLeafSchema(elements, cm, name, parentPath, isRoot);
185      }
186   }
187
188   /** Optional&lt;X&gt; → optional group with "value" child of type X (2.2). */
189   @SuppressWarnings({
190      "java:S3776" // Cognitive Complexity: optional/value dispatch is inherently branchy
191   })
192   private void addOptionalSchema(List<ParquetSchemaElement> elements, ClassMeta<?> cm, String name, String parentPath, boolean isRoot, Object sampleBean, Map<Class<?>, Integer> typesInProgress) {
193      var et = cm.getElementType();
194      if (et == null) // HTT: ClassMeta always resolves Optional's type parameter to Object at minimum
195         et = marshallingContext.getClassMeta(Object.class);
196      Object innerSample = sampleBean instanceof Optional<?> o ? o.orElse(null) : sampleBean;
197      var optPath = parentPath != null ? parentPath + "." + name : name;
198      elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, null, null, isRoot ? null : OPTIONAL, 1, null, null, null, null, optPath));
199      addSchemaElements(elements, et, "value", optPath, false, innerSample, typesInProgress);
200   }
201
202   @SuppressWarnings({
203      "java:S3776" // Cognitive Complexity: bean/cycle/property dispatch is inherently branchy
204   })
205   private void addBeanSchema(List<ParquetSchemaElement> elements, ClassMeta<?> cm, String name, String parentPath, boolean isRoot, Object sampleBean, Map<Class<?>, Integer> typesInProgress) {
206      var bm = cm.getBeanMeta();
207      if (bm == null) // HTT: addBeanSchema is only called after isBean() check; getBeanMeta() is non-null
208         throw illegalArg("Class ''{0}'' is not a bean", cm.getName());
209      var beanClass = cm.inner();
210      // Recursion reached here indirectly (e.g. through Optional/collection of the same type) at the depth
211      // limit: keep the legacy String back-reference placeholder so the enclosing group's child count stays
212      // consistent.  Direct bean-property recursion is handled by the per-property filter below (truncated
213      // by omission, which round-trips as null).
214      if (typesInProgress.getOrDefault(beanClass, 0) >= maxRecursionDepth) {
215         if (cycleHandling == ParquetCycleHandling.THROW) {
216            var path = parentPath != null ? parentPath + "." + name : name; // HTT: parentPath is always non-null when recursion limit is reached at depth ≥ 2
217            throw new SerializeException("Cyclic type reference at ''{0}'' (type ''{1}''). Use @ParentProperty to exclude back-references or set cycleHandling(NULL).", path, beanClass.getName());
218         }
219         addLeafSchema(elements, marshallingContext.getClassMeta(String.class), name, parentPath, isRoot);
220         return;
221      }
222      typesInProgress.merge(beanClass, 1, Integer::sum);
223      try {
224         var ap = marshallingContext.getAnnotationProvider();
225         var props = bm.getProperties().values().stream()
226            .filter(BeanPropertyMeta::canRead)
227            .filter(p -> !isParentProperty(ap, p))
228            .toList();
229         var childParentPath = parentPath != null ? parentPath + "." + name : name;
230         // Resolve each property (incl. Object->sample inference) and drop direct recursive bean properties
231         // that would exceed the depth limit (GAP-13).  Omitting them keeps the schema acyclic; on read the
232         // missing column reconstructs as null (documented-lossy beyond the limit) instead of a toString.
233         var entries = new ArrayList<ResolvedProp>();
234         for (var p : props) {
235            Object childSample = null;
236            if (sampleBean instanceof Map<?, ?> m)
237               childSample = m.get(p.getName());
238            ClassMeta<?> propCm = (ClassMeta<?>) p.getBeanInfo();
239            // Native logical types (GAP-9/10): a java.time temporal property carries a swap, so its
240            // resolved ClassMeta is an Object/String surrogate that hides the concrete type.  Recover the
241            // declared field/getter type so the DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP-micros leaf branches can fire.  Only
242            // overrides when the declared type is one of the native logical types we emit.
243            if (nativeLogicalTypes) {
244               var declared = declaredNativeType(p);
245               if (declared != null)
246                  propCm = marshallingContext.getClassMeta(declared);
247            }
248            // When property type is Object, infer from sample so Map/Collection get proper schema (2.2)
249            if ((propCm == null || propCm.isObject()) && childSample != null) // HTT: propCm is non-null (getBeanInfo always non-null for bean properties)
250               propCm = marshallingContext.getClassMeta(childSample.getClass());
251            // When property type is the abstract java.lang.Number, re-type from a concrete numeric sample so
252            // the column uses the actual physical width (INT32/INT64/DOUBLE) and fractional values survive
253            // (GAP-12).  Only fires when the sample is itself a Number — a swapped property whose raw sample
254            // is non-numeric stays on the lossless INT64 default.
255            else if (propCm != null && propCm.is(Number.class) && childSample instanceof Number) // HTT: propCm is non-null (same invariant as above)
256               propCm = marshallingContext.getClassMeta(childSample.getClass());
257            if (propCm != null && propCm.isBean() && typesInProgress.getOrDefault(propCm.inner(), 0) >= maxRecursionDepth) { // HTT: propCm is non-null
258               if (cycleHandling == ParquetCycleHandling.THROW)
259                  throw new SerializeException("Cyclic type reference at ''{0}.{1}'' (type ''{2}''). Use @ParentProperty to exclude back-references or set cycleHandling(NULL).", childParentPath, p.getName(), propCm.inner().getName());
260               continue;
261            }
262            entries.add(new ResolvedProp(p.getName(), propCm, childSample));
263         }
264         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(
265            name,
266            null,
267            null,
268            isRoot ? null : OPTIONAL,
269            entries.size(),
270            null,
271            null,
272            null,
273            null,
274            childParentPath));
275         for (var e : entries)
276            addSchemaElements(elements, e.cm, e.name, childParentPath, false, e.sample, typesInProgress);
277      } finally {
278         typesInProgress.merge(beanClass, -1, (a, b) -> a + b == 0 ? null : a + b);
279      }
280   }
281
282   private record ResolvedProp(String name, ClassMeta<?> cm, Object sample) {}
283
284   /**
285    * Returns the property's declared field/getter class when it is a binary-native logical type
286    * (java.time temporal, {@link java.math.BigDecimal}, or {@link java.math.BigInteger}); otherwise
287    * <jk>null</jk>.  Used to look past a registered swap whose resolved ClassMeta hides the concrete
288    * type, so the native logical-type leaf branches in {@link #addLeafSchema} can fire (GAP-9/10).
289    */
290   private static Class<?> declaredNativeType(BeanPropertyMeta p) {
291      // Resolve the declared class from the field (preferred) or, for getter-only properties, the getter.
292      var f = p.getField();
293      Class<?> c = f != null ? f.getFieldType().inner() : p.getGetter().getReturnType().inner();
294      if (c == LocalDate.class || c == LocalTime.class || c == OffsetTime.class
295         || c == java.math.BigDecimal.class || c == java.math.BigInteger.class
296         || java.util.Date.class.isAssignableFrom(c) || java.util.Calendar.class.isAssignableFrom(c)
297         || java.time.temporal.Temporal.class.isAssignableFrom(c))
298         return c;
299      return null;
300   }
301
302   private static boolean isParentProperty(AnnotationProvider ap, BeanPropertyMeta p) {
303      var g = p.getGetter();
304      if (g != null && ap.has(ParentProperty.class, g))
305         return true;
306      var s = p.getSetter();
307      if (s != null && ap.has(ParentProperty.class, s))
308         return true;
309      var f = p.getField();
310      return f != null && ap.has(ParentProperty.class, f);
311   }
312
313   private void addListSchema(List<ParquetSchemaElement> elements, ClassMeta<?> cm, String name, String parentPath, boolean isRoot, Object sampleBean, Map<Class<?>, Integer> typesInProgress) {
314      var et = cm.getElementType();
315      if (et == null) // HTT: ClassMeta always provides an element type (at least Object) for list types
316         throw illegalArg("List element type cannot be determined for ''{0}''", cm.getName());
317      // Resolve element type from sample when generics are erased (et is Object) for proper list-of-bean
318      // expansion into leaf columns (e.g. members.list.element.name, members.list.element.age)
319      var sampleCollection = extractSampleCollection(sampleBean);
320      if (ne(sampleCollection)) {
321         var first = sampleCollection.iterator().next();
322         if (first != null)
323            et = marshallingContext.getClassMeta(first.getClass());
324      }
325      var listPath = parentPath != null ? parentPath + "." + name : name;
326      elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, null, null, isRoot ? null : OPTIONAL, 1, CONVERTED_LIST, null, null, null, null));
327      elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement("list", null, null, REPEATED, 1, null, null, null, null, null));
328      Object elementSample = null;
329      if (ne(sampleCollection))
330         elementSample = sampleCollection.iterator().next();
331      addSchemaElements(elements, et, "element", listPath + ".list", false, elementSample, typesInProgress);
332   }
333
334   private static Collection<?> extractSampleCollection(Object sampleBean) {
335      if (sampleBean instanceof Collection<?> c)
336         return c;
337      return Collections.emptyList();
338   }
339
340   private void addMapSchema(List<ParquetSchemaElement> elements, ClassMeta<?> cm, String name, String parentPath, boolean isRoot, Map<Class<?>, Integer> typesInProgress) {
341      var vt = cm.getValueType();
342      // Parquet MAP stores keys as STRING; non-String keys (e.g. Enum) are serialized via toString/name()
343      if (vt == null) // HTT: ClassMeta always provides a value type (at least Object) for map types
344         vt = marshallingContext.getClassMeta(Object.class);
345      var mapPath = parentPath != null ? parentPath + "." + name : name;
346      elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, null, null, isRoot ? null : OPTIONAL, 1, CONVERTED_MAP, null, null, null, null));
347      elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement("key_value", null, null, REPEATED, 2, null, null, null, null, null));
348      elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement("key", TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY, null, REQUIRED, null, CONVERTED_UTF8, LOGICAL_TYPE_STRING, null, null, mapPath + ".key_value.key"));
349      addSchemaElements(elements, vt, "value", mapPath + ".key_value", false, null, typesInProgress);
350   }
351
352   @SuppressWarnings({
353      "java:S3776" // Cognitive Complexity: type-to-Parquet mapping requires many branches
354   })
355   private void addLeafSchema(List<ParquetSchemaElement> elements, ClassMeta<?> cm, String name, String parentPath, boolean isRoot) {
356      int repetition = isRoot ? REQUIRED : OPTIONAL;
357      String path = parentPath != null ? parentPath + "." + name : name;
358
359      if (cm.isBoolean()) {
360         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_BOOLEAN, null, repetition, null, null, null, null, null, path));
361      } else if (cm.is(byte.class) || cm.is(Byte.class) || cm.is(short.class) || cm.is(Short.class) || cm.is(int.class) || cm.is(Integer.class)) {
362         Integer ct;
363         if (cm.is(byte.class) || cm.is(Byte.class))
364            ct = CONVERTED_INT_8;
365         else if (cm.is(short.class) || cm.is(Short.class))
366            ct = CONVERTED_INT_16;
367         else  // int/Integer — the only remaining type the enclosing guard admits.
368            ct = CONVERTED_INT_32;
369         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_INT32, null, repetition, null, ct, null, null, null, path));
370      } else if (cm.is(Number.class)) {
371         // A statically-typed java.lang.Number leaf has no fixed physical width.  Mapping it to INT32 silently
372         // truncates values above 2^31 (e.g. 5_000_000_000L -> 705032704) — GAP-12.  Default to INT64, which
373         // is lossless for every integral Number and keeps the wire value integral.  Integral matters for
374         // round-trip correctness: an ObjectSwap whose swap type is exactly java.lang.Number (e.g.
375         // enum-as-ordinal) round-trips through String.valueOf(...), and "0" parses where "0.0" would not.
376         // When a concrete runtime sample is available (addBeanSchema), the column is re-typed to the actual
377         // subtype (INT32/INT64/DOUBLE) so fractional values are preserved too.  BigInteger/BigDecimal have
378         // their own lossless string mappings and never reach this branch.
379         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_INT64, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_INT_64, null, null, null, path));
380      } else if (cm.is(long.class) || cm.is(Long.class)) {
381         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_INT64, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_INT_64, null, null, null, path));
382      } else if (cm.is(float.class) || cm.is(Float.class)) {
383         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_FLOAT, null, repetition, null, null, null, null, null, path));
384      } else if (cm.is(double.class) || cm.is(Double.class)) {
385         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_DOUBLE, null, repetition, null, null, null, null, null, path));
386      } else if (cm.isAssignableTo(CharSequence.class)) {  // String is a CharSequence, so this covers both.
387         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_UTF8, LOGICAL_TYPE_STRING, null, null, path));
388      } else if (cm.isEnum()) {
389         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_ENUM, null, null, null, path));
390      } else if (cm.isByteArray()) {
391         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY, null, repetition, null, null, null, null, null, path));
392      } else if (nativeLogicalTypes && cm.inner() == LocalDate.class) {
393         // DATE (GAP-10): days since epoch as INT32.  Match on inner() (the concrete class resolved from a
394         // runtime sample) since a swapped property ClassMeta reports neither is(LocalDate) nor isDateOrCalendarOrTemporal.
395         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_INT32, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_DATE, LOGICAL_TYPE_DATE, null, null, path));
396      } else if (nativeLogicalTypes && (cm.inner() == LocalTime.class || cm.inner() == OffsetTime.class)) {
397         // TIME (GAP-10): micros since midnight as INT64 (TIME_MICROS).
398         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_INT64, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_TIME_MICROS, LOGICAL_TYPE_TIME, null, null, path));
399      } else if (nativeLogicalTypes && cm.isDateOrCalendarOrTemporal()) {
400         // TIMESTAMP (GAP-10): micros since epoch as INT64 (TIMESTAMP_MICROS) — sub-millisecond precise.
401         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_INT64, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_TIMESTAMP_MICROS, LOGICAL_TYPE_TIMESTAMP, null, null, path));
402      } else if (cm.isDateOrCalendarOrTemporal() && writeDatesAsTimestamp) {
403         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_INT64, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_TIMESTAMP_MILLIS, LOGICAL_TYPE_TIMESTAMP, null, null, path));
404      } else if (cm.isDateOrCalendarOrTemporal()) {
405         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_UTF8, LOGICAL_TYPE_STRING, null, null, path));
406      } else if (cm.isDuration()) {
407         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_UTF8, LOGICAL_TYPE_STRING, null, null, path));
408      } else if (nativeLogicalTypes && (cm.is(java.math.BigDecimal.class) || cm.is(java.math.BigInteger.class))) {
409         // DECIMAL (GAP-9): unscaled value as INT64 with scale/precision in the schema.  Scale is fixed at
410         // DECIMAL_SCALE; values requiring more fractional digits are rounded (HALF_UP) on write.
411         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_INT64, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_DECIMAL, LOGICAL_TYPE_DECIMAL, DECIMAL_SCALE, DECIMAL_PRECISION, path));
412      } else if (cm.isAssignableTo(UUID.class)) {
413         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_FIXED_LEN_BYTE_ARRAY, 16, repetition, null, null, LOGICAL_TYPE_UUID, null, null, path));
414      } else {
415         elements.add(new ParquetSchemaElement(name, TYPE_BYTE_ARRAY, null, repetition, null, CONVERTED_UTF8, LOGICAL_TYPE_STRING, null, null, path));
416      }
417   }
418
419   /**
420    * Returns leaf (primitive) schema elements suitable for column chunk writing.
421    *
422    * @param schema The full schema from buildSchema.
423    * @return List of leaf elements with non-null paths.
424    */
425   public static List<ParquetSchemaElement> getLeafColumns(List<ParquetSchemaElement> schema) {
426      var leaves = new ArrayList<ParquetSchemaElement>();
427      for (var e : schema)
428         if (e.isLeaf())
429            leaves.add(e);
430      return leaves;
431   }
432}
